channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: South Korea's Yoon defends his martial law decree in his first public appearance since his arrest
item.description: South Korea’s impeached president has denied that he ordered the military to drag lawmakers out of the National Assembly to prevent them from voting to reject his martial law decree last month, as he appeared for the first time before the Constitutional Court that will determine his fate.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Landslides and flash floods on Indonesia’s Java island leave 17 dead and 8 missing
item.description: JAKARTA, Indonesia (AP) — Indonesian rescuers recovered the bodies of at least 17 people who were swept away in flash floods or buried under tons of mud and rocks that hit hilly villages on the country’s main island of Java, officials said Tuesday. Eight people were missing.
item.dc:creator: NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's first day actions loom large as Davos begins
item.description: DAVOS, Switzerland (AP) — As the World Economic Forum’s annual gabfest gets into full swing Tuesday, U.S. President Donald Trump gave everybody something to talk about with his actions on his first day back in office. Energy industry executives will mull Trump's vow to “drill, baby, drill.
item.dc:creator: JAMEY KEATEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taliban say 2 Americans held in Afghanistan were freed in a prisoner exchange
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — A prisoner swap between the United States and Afghanistan's Taliban freed two Americans in exchange for a Taliban figure imprisoned for life in California on drug trafficking and terrorism charges, officials said Tuesday. The deal came as Joe Biden, who oversaw the chaotic U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Strong earthquake in Taiwan injures 27 and causes scattered damage
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A 6.4-magnitude earthquake struck southern Taiwan early Tuesday, leaving 27 people with minor injuries and some reported damage. The quake hit at 12:17 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Panama, familiar with US intervention, bristles at Trump's comments on canal
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump’s insistence that he wants to have the Panama Canal back under U.S. control is feeding nationalist sentiment and worry in Panama, home to the critical trade route and a country familiar with U.S. military intervention.
item.dc:creator: Juan Zamorano, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump seeks to designate drug cartels as foreign terrorist organizations
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — President Donald Trump signed an executive order Monday saying the United States would designate drug cartels as foreign terrorist organizations in a move that could push a militarized agenda for the border and Latin America.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China's population falls for a third straight year, posing challenges for its government and economy
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China's population fell last year for the third straight year, its government said Friday, pointing to further demographic challenges for the world's second most populous nation, which is now facing both an aging population and an emerging shortage of working age people.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada says it is ready to respond if Trump follows through with imposing sweeping tariffs Feb. 1
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Top Canadian ministers said Monday that Canada will be ready to retaliate after President Donald Trump said he was thinking of imposing a 25% tariff on Canada and Mexico on Feb. 1. Trump has been threatening to impose sweeping tariffs on Canada, Mexico and other trading partners.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yemen's Houthis signal they'll now limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to Israeli ships
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels signaled Monday they now will limit their attacks in the Red Sea corridor to only Israeli-affiliated ships after a ceasefire began in the Gaza Strip, but warned wider assaults could resume if needed.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio unanimously confirmed as secretary of State, becoming Trump's first Cabinet official
item.description: The Senate on Monday unanimously confirmed Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) as America's 72nd secretary of State, putting in place the first member of President Trump's Cabinet on the day of his inauguration. Rubio’s confirmation vote passed with overwhelming bipartisan support, 99-0.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Colombia's president will declare an emergency over deadly ELN attacks in the northeast
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro says he will declare an emergency over the guerrilla attacks in the northeast that have killed dozens of people and forced thousands to flee.
item.dc:creator: Manuel Rueda And Astrid Suárez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Israeli president calls Trump a 'true friend' on Inauguration Day, praises his work to release hostages
item.description: Israeli President Isaac Herzog is praising Donald Trump as a "true friend" of Israel, thanking him for his "commitment to bringing all our hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Poland wants the EU focused on security. Its border with Belarus highlights the challenges
item.description: POLOWCE, Poland (AP) — Poland's six-month presidency of the European Union is firmly focused on security. As Europe’s biggest land war in decades rages, fewer places highlight the challenges and contradictions of defending the bloc and its values more starkly than the border with Belarus.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli American hostage father says he has ‘immense gratitude’ for Trump team after ceasefire deal
item.description: An Israeli American hostage’s father said Sunday that he has “immense gratitude” for President-elect Trump’s team in the wake of a ceasefire deal in the Israel-Hamas war going into effect. “You're going to be attending the inauguration tomorrow.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel releases 90 Palestinian prisoners after Hamas frees 3 Israeli hostages
item.description: Israel has freed 90 Palestinian prisoners and detainees. The release came early Monday, more than seven hours after three Israeli hostages released from Hamas captivity in Gaza returned to Israel.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Palestinians trek across rubble to return to their homes as Gaza ceasefire takes hold
item.description: KHAN YOUNIS, Gaza Strip (AP) — Even before the ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas was fully in place on Sunday, Palestinians in the war-battered Gaza Strip began to return to the remains of the homes they had evacuated during the 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Graham calls for the 'decimation' of Iranian nuclear program after ceasefire
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.) on Sunday called for the “decimation” of the Iranian nuclear program in the wake of a new ceasefire in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Nigerian gasoline tanker explosion death toll rises to 86, with 55 others injured
item.description: ABUJA, Nigeria (AP) — The death toll from a gasoline tanker explosion in north-central Nigeria has risen to 86, the country’s emergency response agency said on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: DYEPKAZAH SHIBAYAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson vows there will be 'hell to pay' if Hamas violates ceasefire deal
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) vowed that there will be “hell to pay” if Hamas violates the ceasefire deal struck recently with Israel. Johnson joined NBC News’s “Meet the Press” on Sunday, saying he was hopeful about the truce announced last week.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump wants to visit China, strengthen relationship: Report
item.description: The president-elect has vowed to impose tariffs and other mechanisms against China upon his return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza ceasefire takes hold and the first handover of hostages is underway
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hamas took hold in the Gaza Strip on Sunday, ushering in an initial six-week period of calm and raising hopes for the release of dozens of militant-held hostages and an end to the devastating 15-month war.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: German ambassador warns Trump will 'undermine' democratic principles with 'maximum disruption' agenda: report
item.description: In a confidential briefing document, Germany's ambassador to the United States Andreas Michaelis warned that Donald Trump will "undermine" democratic principles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases 3 hostages under Israel cease-fire deal
item.description: Three female hostages were freed by Hamas under the terms of an Israeli cease-fire agreement, which was delayed by several hours Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to expect as Israel-Hamas cease-fire goes into effect on Sunday
item.description: The Israeli government approved the deal early Saturday morning for a cease-fire in Gaza and the release of hostages captured after Hamas' terrorist attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel, Hamas to go into effect Sunday morning, Qatar says
item.description: The cease-fire between Hamas and Israel is expected to go into effect on Sunday at 8:30 a.m. local time, Qatar’s foreign ministry announced Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Cabinet approves a deal for a ceasefire in Gaza and the release of dozens of hostages
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s Cabinet approved a deal early Saturday for a ceasefire in Gaza that would release dozens of hostages held there and pause the 15-month war with Hamas, bringing the sides a step closer to ending their deadliest and most destructive fighting ever.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, WAFAA SHURAFA and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ancient Pompeii excavation uncovers lavish private bath complex
item.description: A private bath complex in Pompeii has been unearthed by archaeologists; the baths highlight the wealth of the ancient city before it was destroyed in AD 79.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexican influencers reportedly killed for ties to Sinaloa cartel
item.description: Bloodshed between members of the Sinaloa cartel may be common, but the death of influencers is a new development.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Brazilian court denies Bolsonaro’s request to travel to Trump’s inauguration
item.description: Brazil’s Supreme Court has denied former President Jair Bolsonaro's request to have his passport temporarily restored in order to attend the inauguration of U.S. President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two Americans to be released from Gaza in first phase of ceasefire deal
item.description: Two Americans, Keith Siegel and Sagui Dekel-Chen, are expected to be released as part of the first phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal that emerged Wednesday after being held for 15-months by Hamas since the start of the war in Gaza, according to a senior administration official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Ceasefire in Gaza begins, Hamas releases hostage names to Israel
item.description: The ceasefire was delayed but will take effect Sunday after Hamas released the list of hostage names to Israeli officials.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: South Korean authorities take impeached President Yoon to detention center after questioning
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea's impeached president, Yoon Suk Yeol, was sent to a detention center near Seoul on Wednesday night, after being questioned by anti-corruption officials who took him into custody over his imposition of martial law last month.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hamas OKs draft agreement of a Gaza ceasefire and the release of some hostages, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages, two officials involved in the talks said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Polish town invites Musk to buy castle for his European HQ
item.description: Tech billionaire is reportedly looking for a sizeable location, possibly a castle in Italy, as a hub for his operations in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation
